title: Make a Phone Call Confidently
summary: Make a phone call without anxiety, knowing what to say.
learnerPromise: Make a phone call without anxiety, knowing what to say.
whyItMatters: Phone calls are still necessary for appointments, emergencies, and job opportunities. Confidence on the phone opens doors.
realLifeUses: Call a doctor's office to book an appointment.
Phone a store to check if an item is in stock.
Call a landlord to report a problem.
Follow up on a job application by phone.
youWillLearn: State the purpose of your call.
End the call with a thank you.
miniChallenge: Call a store or restaurant and ask one simple question, like their hours.
steps: Before calling, write down what you need to ask.
Greet the person and say your name.
State why you are calling in one sentence.
Ask your question or make your request.
Thank the person and say goodbye before hanging up.
completionCriteria: You make a call from start to finish without hanging up early.
You get the information or result you needed.
commonProblems: Feeling nervous and forgetting what to say.
Talking too fast or too quietly.
Not having details ready when asked.
tips: Practice what you will say out loud before dialing.
Smile while you talk. It makes your voice sound friendlier.
Have a pen and paper ready to take notes.
